# BEFORE THE DISTRICT CONSUMER DISPUTES REDRESSAL COMMISSION, PUNE DISTRICT, MAHARASHTRA

---

**COMPLAINT NO.:** *(To be allotted by the Commission)*

---

**IN THE MATTER OF:**

**Ramesh Kumar,**
S/o. Late [Father's Name],
Residing at [Full Address],
Pune, Maharashtra,
Mobile: [Contact Number],
Email: [Email Address],
...(hereinafter referred to as the **"Complainant"**)

**VERSUS**

**Suresh Electronics (represented through its Sole Proprietor/Partner/Authorized Signatory)**
Having its place of business at Kothrud, Pune, Maharashtra,
...(hereinafter referred to as the **"Opposite Party"**)

---

## SUBJECT: COMPLAINT UNDER SECTION 35 OF THE CONSUMER PROTECTION ACT, 2019 FOR DEFICIENCY IN SERVICE AND UNFAIR TRADE PRACTICE

---

**Respectfully submitted before this Hon'ble Commission the following:**

---

## 1. FACTS OF THE COMPLAINT

The Complainant most respectfully submits the following facts giving rise to the present complaint:

**1.** That the Complainant is a law-abiding citizen and a consumer as defined under Section 2(42) of the Consumer Protection Act, 2019 (hereinafter referred to as "the Act"). The Complainant purchased goods for personal use and not for commercial purposes.

**2.** That on or about the **10th day of March, 2025**, the Complainant approached the Opposite Party's establishment, namely "Suresh Electronics," situated at Kothrud, Pune, Maharashtra, for the purpose of purchasing a new Samsung Television.

**3.** That based on the specific assurance and representation made by the Opposite Party that the Samsung Television being sold was a brand new, unused, and unopened product, the Complainant purchased the said television for a sum of **Rupees Forty-Five Thousand Only (Rs. 45,000/-)**.

**4.** That the Complainant made the payment of Rs. 45,000/- through bank transfer, the details of which are available with the Complainant, and received a purchase bill/invoice dated 10th March 2025 from the Opposite Party.

**5.** That subsequently, when the Complainant inspected the Samsung Television more closely or used the same, the Complainant discovered and had reasonable grounds to believe that the television supplied was **not a new product as assured and represented, but was in fact a refurbished, pre-owned, or used model**.

**6.** That the Complainant, upon discovering the aforementioned defect and misrepresentation, approached the Opposite Party at his shop at Kothrud, Pune, and confronted him with the fact that the television was a refurbished product despite being sold as a new product.

**7.** That during the course of such confrontation, the Opposite Party, when confronted by the Complainant, **admitted on a video recording** that the Samsung Television supplied was indeed a refurbished model and not a brand new product as originally represented and sold.

**8.** That the Complainant then requested the Opposite Party for a full refund of Rs. 45,000/- in lieu of the defective and misrepresented product supplied.

**9.** That the Opposite Party, instead of addressing the Complainant's legitimate grievance, **wrongfully refused the request for refund** and furthermore, the Complainant states with anguish that the Opposite Party **threatened and intimidated the Complainant** when approached for resolution.

**10.** That the Opposite Party has failed and neglected to either replace the product with a genuine new product or refund the amount despite repeated requests and demands made by the Complainant.

**11.** That the Opposite Party has engaged in unfair trade practice by misrepresenting the nature and quality of the goods sold, thereby cheating and deceiving the Complainant.

**12.** That the Complainant has suffered financial loss, mental agony, harassment, and undue stress on account of the deficient and unfair conduct of the Opposite Party.

---

## 2. DEFICIENCY AND UNFAIR TRADE PRACTICE

The Complainant states that the Opposite Party is guilty of the following:

**(a) Deficiency in Service:** The Opposite Party failed to deliver a brand new product as promised and assured at the time of sale, thereby committing deficiency in terms of Section 2(38) of the Act.

**(b) Unfair Trade Practice:** The Opposite Party misrepresented the Samsung Television as a brand new product when it was actually a refurbished model, which constitutes an unfair trade practice under Section 2(47) of the Act, specifically falling within the purview of Section 2(47)(i) and/or other relevant sub-clauses.

**(c) Deficiency in Sale of Goods:** The goods supplied were not of merchantable quality and were not fit for the purpose for which they were purchased, namely as a brand new television set.

---

## 3. LEGAL GROUNDS

The Complainant humbly submits that this Hon'ble Commission has jurisdiction to entertain and try this complaint under **Section 38 of the Consumer Protection Act, 2019**, as the value of goods and compensation claimed does not exceed rupees seventy-five lakhs, and the cause of action has arisen within the territorial jurisdiction of this Commission.

The Complainant relies upon, inter alia, the following provisions of the Consumer Protection Act, 2019:

**(i) Section 2(38):** Defining "Deficiency" to mean a fault, imperfection, shortcoming, or inadequacy in the quality, nature, and manner of performance which is required to be maintained by or under any law for the time being in force or under any contract, or otherwise in relation to any service;

**(ii) Section 2(42):** Defining "Consumer" to include a person who buys any goods for a consideration which has been paid or promised or partly paid and partly promised, or under any system of deferred payment;

**(iii) Section 2(47):** Defining "Unfair Trade Practice" to include the practice of misrepresenting goods as new goods when they are not so;

**(iv) Section 35:** Cognizance of complaints by the District Commission;

**(v) Section 38:** Jurisdiction of the District Commission;

**(vi) Section 39:** Power of District Commission to cease to entertain the complaint after transfer;

**(vii) Section 46:** Power to grant relief;

**(viii) Section 57:** Findings of the District Commission; and

**(ix) Section 58:** Penalty for non-compliance of order passed by the District Commission;

**(x)** Further, the Complainant craves leave to rely upon such other provisions, judgments, and legal precedents as may be applicable from time to time.

---

## 4. DOCUMENTS ANNEXED

The following documents are annexed herewith and form part and parcel of this complaint:

| **Sr. No.** | **Description of Document** | **Mark as** |
|-------------|-----------------------------|-------------|
| 1. | Original purchase bill/invoice dated 10th March 2025 issued by Suresh Electronics | **Annexure A** |
| 2. | Bank transfer receipt/confirmation of payment of Rs. 45,000/- | **Annexure B** |
| 3. | Video recording of the Opposite Party (Suresh Electronics) admitting that the Samsung Television supplied is a refurbished product | **Annexure C** |
| 4. | Copy of the Complainant's Aadhaar Card/Identity Proof | **Annexure D** |
| 5. | Copy of Complainant's Address Proof | **Annexure E** |

*The originals of the aforementioned documents shall be produced for verification before this Hon'ble Commission as and when required.*

---

## 5. RELIEF SOUGHT

The Complainant, through this complaint, humbly prays that this Hon'ble Commission may be pleased to pass the following orders and reliefs:

**(i)** Direct the Opposite Party to **refund the entire amount of Rs. 45,000/-** (Rupees Forty-Five Thousand Only) paid by the Complainant towards the purchase of the misrepresented Samsung Television;

**(ii)** Direct the Opposite Party to pay **compensation for mental agony and harassment** suffered by the Complainant on account of the deficient and unfair conduct of the Opposite Party;

**(iii
